mapping.b_element: scale-free network nodes (high-degree hubs and low-degree nodes)
low-degree nodes (less critical for global connectivity)

mapping.a_element: evolutionary cohesion and rate differences between core and periphery
mapping.b_element: differential impact of random failures vs. targeted attacks on hubs

brief_justification: While both papers analyze network topology and its functional implications, Paper A focuses on modularity and co-evolutionary dynamics in metabolic networks, whereas Paper B examines robustness and vulnerability in scale-free networks under error/attack scenarios. The underlying mechanisms (evolutionary modularity vs. error/attack tolerance) are distinct, despite superficial overlap in network terminology.
